checks.finding: Core terms are concretely bound: loop-gate and build job are defined in Field Definitions (requirements.md:243-253); release surfaces are enumerated in Problems (18 plugin manifests, both marketplaces, README.md, tests/validate-repository.ps1, tests/repository-release-validation.tests.sh); REQ-001/REQ-002 cite exact file:line anchors (scripts/bump-version.sh:51,58; release.yml:32). No remaining term (fixture, release surface, loop-gate, build job) is left to two divergent implementer readings.

The round-1 CHANGELOG.md ownership conflict is resolved: Main Workflows item 1 has T-001 CREATE the single #148 entry, item 2 has T-002 APPEND to the SAME entry (requirements.md:270-282), and Edge Cases' shared-files paragraph states 'a single shared entry, never two parallel entries, matching Main Workflows items 1-2' (requirements.md:319-326), serialized by T-002's Blocker on T-001. AC-011 references 'an entry' (singular), matching. Independent re-read of Goals, Non-goals, Roles, Security Boundaries, and Risks found no other direct conflicts (e.g., release.yml trigger-surface language in Non-goals/Edge Cases is consistent; REQ-004/AC-013/AC-014 bash-only decision is consistent across Goals, Non-goals, and User Stories).

Material failure modes implied by the Problems/Goals are each mapped to a negative-path AC: single-suite failure independently for both suites (AC-002/AC-003, zero-mutation via git status --porcelain), bypass absence (AC-004), ordering (AC-005), weakened-gate/continue-on-error escape hatch with a non-vacuous negative canary (AC-008/AC-009), and CI-resilience for the two new suites (AC-006, Edge Cases first bullet). The release.yml trigger-surface edge case (a release published from a commit that never ran test.yml) is explicitly called out and shown to still be gated by the independent loop-gate job (requirements.md:313-318).

All seven Open Questions (OQ-001..OQ-007) carry an explicit RESOLVED decision with rationale (requirements.md:358-377), matching investigation.md's default recommendations. The four Assumptions (suite fail-closed behavior, _PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES stability, the text-marker technique's continued validity, and the pillar-a REQ-006 rule) are stable environmental facts observed at investigation time, not undecided product behavior that would block task decomposition.

The spec's core boundary is an irreversible-change gate: release-surface mutation (bump-version.sh) and the tarball/SBOM/checksum/attestation/upload chain (release.yml) must not proceed unless both loop suites exit 0, with 'No environment variable, CLI flag, or other bypass' (REQ-001) and no continue-on-error/if:always() escape hatch (REQ-002). This boundary is directly testable via AC-002/AC-003 (zero-mutation), AC-004 (no bypass conditional), and AC-008/AC-009 (weakened-gate negative scan with a non-vacuous canary), so the boundary is not merely asserted but locked by acceptance tests.

Every Goal (REQ-001..REQ-004) maps to a task (T-001/T-002), every AC maps 1:1 to a TEST ID with concrete test type and target, Non-goals bound the scope against the sibling risk-adaptive-layer spec (INV-015) and the protected-gate table, and Roles/Security Boundaries are filled in. The one deferred item — the concrete mechanism enforcing 'T-002's Blocker on T-001' — is explicitly pointed at design.md's Global Constraints section, which is an implementation-mechanism detail properly left to Phase 2 per the calibration's Phase-1-only gate scope, not a missing product-behavior decision.
